channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Alberta Court Quashes Independence Petition Due to First Nations Treaty Concerns
item.description: An Alberta Court of King’s Bench justice has nullified Elections Alberta's approval of a petition seeking a referendum on Alberta independence, initiated by the separatist group Stay Free Alberta. The...

item.title: Wes Streeting plans to initiate Labour leadership contest amid party divisions
item.description: Wes Streeting, the health secretary, is reportedly preparing to launch a challenge to Keir Starmer's leadership of the Labour Party, provided he secures the backing of enough MPs. This potential leade...

item.title: Supreme Court allows TVK MLA to vote in Tamil Nadu floor test, stays Madras HC order
item.description: In a crucial development ahead of the Tamil Nadu assembly floor test, the Supreme Court on Wednesday allowed MLA Srinivasa Sethupathi of the TVK party to participate, staying the Madras High Court ord...

item.title: PM Modi Urges Work From Home Voluntarily; No Official Mandate Issued, Say Sources
item.description: According to government sources, Prime Minister Narendra Modi has appealed to citizens and businesses to consider working from home where feasible, as a precautionary measure. However, there is no for...

item.title: Iheanacho’s Last-Minute Penalty Keeps Celtic in Scottish Premiership Title Race Against Hearts
item.description: Na so drama take dey for Scottish Premiership this season. Kelechi Iheanacho score one controversial penalty for 99th minute wey carry Celtic go win 3-2 against Motherwell. Dis win mean say Celtic sti...

item.title: Police confiscate five kilos of shabu worth P34 million in Zamboanga City operation
item.description: Authorities in Zamboanga City successfully confiscated five kilos of shabu valued at P34 million during an entrapment operation on May 12, 2026. The operation, conducted in the underground parking are...

item.title: Tripura government orders 50% work-from-home for employees following PM’s appeal
item.description: In response to an appeal made by the Prime Minister, the Tripura government has decided to implement a 50 percent work-from-home policy for its employees. This step has been taken as a precautionary m...

item.title: Rising Risks from Reentering Space Debris Due to Advanced Spacecraft Materials
item.description: As space launches increase worldwide, reentering space debris is becoming a growing safety concern. Components like nonreusable rocket boosters and decommissioned satellites are meant to burn up upon ...

item.title: KUSCCO Faces Sh12 Billion Deficit Following Years of Financial Mismanagement, Says Oparanya
item.description: Co-operatives and MSMEs Development Cabinet Secretary Wycliffe Oparanya has disclosed that the Kenya Union of Savings and Credit Co-operatives (KUSCCO) is experiencing a massive financial crisis estim...

item.title: Manitoba considers regulator to oversee social media and AI restrictions for children under 16
item.description: Manitoba Premier Wab Kinew announced plans to potentially appoint a commissioner or regulator to enforce a proposed ban on social media platforms and AI chatbots for children under 16. The aim is to p...

item.title: Visa customers participate in contest to win a luxury Mauritius trip
item.description: Tanzania Insight reports say Visa card users now get chance to enter competition wey fit carry dem go one luxury trip for Mauritius. The contest dey aimed at rewarding loyal customers of Visa wey dey ...

item.title: Zanzibar Lawmakers Raise Concerns Over Governance and Public Service Efficiency
item.description: Zanzibar legislators have raised serious concerns over weak governance and accountability within the public service. During a debate on the Ministry of Constitution, Legal Affairs, Public Service, and...

item.title: Lisa Murray Explores Healing Through Photography in 'This Too Shall Pass'
item.description: Lisa Murray, an Australian multimedia artist, presents her latest photography project, 'This Too Shall Pass,' which reflects her journey of healing from illness and trauma. Murray uses photography, sc...

item.title: ANC councillors voice concerns over slow SIU investigation and poor service delivery in Sedibeng
item.description: ANC councillors from the Sedibeng district municipality have publicly expressed serious concerns about the lack of progress by the Special Investigating Unit (SIU) in probing corruption and maladminis...

item.title: PDP Adjusts Schedule for Pre-Election Activities Ahead of 2027 Elections
item.description: The Peoples Democratic Party (PDP) don announce say dem don change some dates for their pre-election activities as dem dey prepare for the 2027 general elections. According to the statement wey the Na...

item.title: Concerns Raised Over Ekurhuleni City Manager Lerutla’s Return Amid Legal Charges
item.description: The Democratic Alliance (DA) in Ekurhuleni has expressed outrage at mayor Nkosindiphile Xhakaza’s decision to allow city manager Kagiso Lerutla to return to work, despite facing serious legal charges ...

item.title: Alberto Vázquez Explores Reality and Midlife Crisis in Animated Film 'Decorado'
item.description: Spanish animator Alberto Vázquez has released his latest animated feature, 'Decorado', which blends elements of comedy and drama to explore deep philosophical questions. The film centers on a mouse wh...

item.title: Canadian Astronaut Jeremy Hansen Shares Lessons on Failure, Space Missions, and International Collaboration
item.description: Canadian astronaut Jeremy Hansen spoke to an audience at Ottawa's National Arts Centre about the importance of persistence and overcoming challenges. Drawing from his recent experience on the Artemis ...

item.title: Kenya Police Launch Tech-Driven Measures and Training Ahead of 2027 Elections
item.description: The National Police Service (NPS) of Kenya has announced a major overhaul in preparation for the 2027 General Election. Inspector General Douglas Kanja told Parliament that the service is investing he...

item.title: Rassie Erasmus names 40-man Springbok alignment squad ahead of international season
item.description: Springbok coach Rassie Erasmus has announced a 40-man squad for an alignment camp next week, preparing for the international season starting in late June while also scouting talent for the 2027 World ...
